chapter_context.summary: Describes a catastrophic plague of four waves of locusts that completely strips the land of vines and fig trees, resulting in severe agricultural famine, causing the priests, farmers, and vinedressers to mourn, and prompting a call to a solemn fast and assembly in the house of the Lord.

outline.text: The Devastating Locust Plague: Four Waves of Destruction (Palmerworm, Locust, Cankerworm, Caterpillar)
outline.explanation: This section focuses on The Devastating Locust Plague: Four Waves of Destruction (Palmerworm, Locust, Cankerworm, Caterpillar) (1:1-4), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Joel.

outline.text: The Call to Mourn: Drunkards, Priests, Husbandmen, and Vinedressers Bereft of Crop and Oblation
outline.explanation: This section focuses on The Call to Mourn: Drunkards, Priests, Husbandmen, and Vinedressers Bereft of Crop and Oblation (1:5-12), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Joel.

outline.text: Call to a Solemn Fast and Cry to God as the Day of the Lord Draws Nigh with Famine and Fire
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Call to a Solemn Fast and Cry to God as the Day of the Lord Draws Nigh with Famine and Fire (1:13-20), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Joel.

key_themes.title: The Day of the Lord
key_themes.description: A central prophetic theme denoting a time of God's direct, sovereign intervention in human history for judgment and salvation.

key_themes.title: Genuine Inward Repentance
key_themes.description: Joel emphasizes that external rituals (garment-rending) are useless without sincere change of heart and turning to God.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the warnings and invitations of Chapter 1, what does it mean to rend your heart rather than your garments in your walk with God?
How does the promise of the outpouring of the Holy Spirit and cosmic salvation in this chapter bring hope and confidence to your daily faith?

cultural_historical.title: Locust Plagues in the Ancient Near East
cultural_historical.text: Swarms of locusts were a frequent and devastating threat to ancient agrarian economies, capable of stripping entire regions of vegetation in hours, which Joel uses as a powerful metaphor for invading armies and divine judgment.
